name: Gerdar MTB Route
name_original: Esterri d'Àneu a El Gerdar — circular por Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici
description: This difficult 21.0-mile mountain biking route through Aigüestortes National Park offers stunning Pyrenean landscapes and a unique walking s
region_intro: Hiking around Esterri D'Àneu offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Pallars Sobirà region in the Catalan Pyrenees. The area is characterized by dramatic mountains, deep valleys, and the Noguera Pallaresa river system, which includes numerous glacial lakes and reservoirs. It serves as a gateway to the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, featuring alpine terrain and towering peaks. Best hiking trails in Esterri D'Àneu The most popular hiking route is Esterri d'Àneu - Isavarre - València d'Àneu Loop, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete, passing through charming villages and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pont d'Isavarre – View of the Àneu Valley loop from Esterri d'Àneu, a moderate 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Àneu Valley and surrounding mountains. Local hikers also love the Abandoned Village of Dorve – Pantà de la Torrassa loop from Esterri d'Àneu, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through historical sites and alongside the Pantà de la Torrassa reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Esterri D'Àneu is defined by its Pyrenean setting, alpine lakes, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging multi-day treks. The routes in Esterri D'Àneu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 460 reviews. More than 3,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Esterri D'Àneu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Esterri D'Àneu?
A: Esterri D'Àneu offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking routes. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to challenging multi-day treks, allowing you to explore the dramatic Pyrenean landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Esterri D'Àneu?
A: The terrain around Esterri D'Àneu is characterized by its dramatic Pyrenean setting. You'll encounter varied landscapes including lush forests, alpine meadows, river valleys, and significant elevation changes as you ascend into the mountains. Many routes also feature glacial lakes and reservoirs, such as the Pantà de la Torrassa.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Esterri D'Àneu?
A: Yes, Esterri D'Àneu has several easy routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Esterri d'Àneu&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.1 km path that takes about 1 hour 30 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's beauty.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see stunning alpine lakes, winding rivers like the Noguera Pallaresa, and towering peaks. The area is a gateway to the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, home to the emblematic Encantats peaks. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Estany de Sant Maurici&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2579934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monestero Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Esterri D'Àneu?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Esterri D'Àneu are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20528290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Peter in Jou – Pantà de la Torrassa loop from Esterri d'Àneu&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 15.7 km and takes around 5 hours.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Esterri D'Àneu?
A: Esterri D'Àneu offers year-round appeal. Hiking is most popular during the warmer months, typically from late spring through early autumn, when trails are clear of snow and the weather is mild. However, the region also provides opportunities for snow activities and winter hiking, offering a different perspective on its natural beauty.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, the diverse range of trails includes options suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Esterri d'Àneu&lt;/a&gt; suggests that shorter, less strenuous paths are available for families to enjoy together.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Esterri D'Àneu?
A: The hiking trails in Esterri D'Àneu are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 460 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning Pyrenean scenery, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that lead through charming villages and to breathtaking viewpoints.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Esterri D'Àneu and the surrounding villages offer traditional rural architecture and amenities. While specific cafes or pubs on trails are not listed, you can find accommodation and places to eat within the town and nearby charming villages, providing a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventures.

Q: Are there any mountain huts or refuges in the area for multi-day hikes?
A: Yes, the region around Esterri D'Àneu, particularly as a gateway to Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, features several mountain huts. These are essential for longer, multi-day treks like the Carros de Foc. Notable refuges include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/567109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Josep Maria Blanc Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amitges Refuge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Esterri D'Àneu?
A: Hikes around Esterri D'Àneu vary significantly in duration. Easy loops can be completed in as little as 1.5 hours, while moderate trails might take 3-5 hours. More challenging and difficult routes, especially those with significant elevation gain or leading to remote areas, can take 5 hours or more, and multi-day treks are also possible.
tips: Ruins of the Castle of Valencia de Àneu. Cultural heritage with great history. We will walk the path that takes us up to the top and enjoy the magnificent views of the Àneu valley.

The Gerdar forest is located in the Pallarés valley. Legend has it that it is enchanted and its inhabitants are elves called "minairons" who live in the hollows of the trunks. It is said that if they appear on your way they will tell you "que parlem que farem" (which means "let's talk about what we'll do") and that you have to give them a task if you don't want them to bother you during the tour.

The forest is very beautiful and easy to walk. End at the Gerber viewpoint from where you can see the impressive Collimar waterfall.

It is located in a wide valley of glacial origin, in the first widening that Noguera Pallaresa finds when leaving behind the closed Vall d'Isil. The town is located in a flat area between the Noguera Pallaresa and the Rio de Son, at the foot of the south-western foothills of the Faro. Esterri d'Àneu has the smallest municipality in the Pallars Sobirà region and, on the other hand, the second most populated, after the head of the region, Sort.

Despite not being a very large population (it does not reach 1,000 inhabitants), it is truly the capital of the Vall d'Àneu, both for services (medical, educational, commercial, etc.) and for being the natural meeting point for the whole of towns in the four municipalities of the valley: Alt Àneu, Espot, Esterri d'Àneu and Guingueta d'Àneu. It should be noted, for example, that in all of Pallars Sobirà there are only two public secondary education centers: Sort and Esterri d'Àneu.
It preserves a cross at the top of Carrer Major which, according to tradition, dates from 1633, made by the French. There is also the Pont d'Esterri d'Àneu, Romanesque, with two eyes, above the Noguera Pallaresa.

According to Joan Coromines, Esterri is one of the many Pyrenean toponyms of Basque origin. Two Basque roots come together, eto (fence, full) and erri (place, place): the place of the fence. The second part of the toponym refers to the valley where it is located: Vall d'Àneu. The toponym "Àneu" also comes from the pre-Roman anabi, meaning blueberry.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Esterri d'Àneu to El Gerdar mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as difficult by the Valls d'Àneu MTB Centre. It requires a good level of fitness and advanced riding skills due to its significant elevation changes (around 860m up and down) and varied terrain, which can include technical sections, high gradients, and potentially loose or rocky surfaces. It is not suitable for beginners.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 33.7 km long. While the estimated moving time is around 2 hours and 55 minutes, you should plan for a longer overall duration, typically 4-6 hours, to account for breaks, photo opportunities, and the mandatory 1 km section where you'll need to walk your bike.

faqs.question: Where does the Esterri d'Àneu to El Gerdar route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route begins in Esterri d'Àneu. As a common starting point for routes in the Valls d'Àneu area, you can typically find parking within the town. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors and outdoor enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for mountain biking within Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park?
faqs.answer: While the route borders and traverses parts of the National Park, it's crucial to respect park regulations. A notable rule on this specific trail is a mandatory 1 km section within the Gerdar Forest where you must dismount and walk your bike to protect the natural environment. Generally, mountain biking is restricted to designated paths within the park, and off-trail riding is prohibited. There are no specific entrance fees for the park itself, but always check for any updated local regulations or permit requirements for certain activities.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the buffer zones and some areas of Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, specific regulations apply, especially within the core protected areas. It's best to check the latest park rules regarding dogs on trails, particularly for a route that enters sensitive forest areas like the Gerdar Forest, where wildlife protection is paramount. Dogs are usually required to be on a leash.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Esterri d'Àneu to El Gerdar route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is typically from late spring (May/June) through early autumn (September/October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and snow has usually melted from higher elevations. Summer offers warmer temperatures, but afternoon thunderstorms can occur. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially in the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied. You'll start on a tarmacked trail from Esterri d'Àneu, transition to roads towards La Bonaigua mountain pass, and then encounter trails across the La Mata glade and through the Gerdar Forest. Expect a mix of surfaces including loose gravel, rocky sections, dirt paths, and potentially muddy or grassy areas, especially in higher elevations. The route also uses parts of the old La Bonaigua highway and the C-28 road.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by the ruins of València d'Àneu Castle, offering great views of the Àneu Valley. The route also takes you through the beautiful Gerdar Forest, known for its ancient firs, and provides stunning vistas of the high-mountain landscapes bordering Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, with its jagged peaks and alpine meadows.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Gedar (57% of the route), Aran Àneu (48%), Ramat de Camins. Etapa 10 : Esterri d'Àneu => Salardú (46%), and Pedals de Foc (44%) trails, among others. This offers a chance to experience segments of these broader networks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers for changing mountain weather. A helmet is essential, and protective gear like gloves and knee pads are highly recommended. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies, and consider a power bank.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Esterri d'Àneu, where you'll find various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops to stock up on supplies before your ride or relax afterward. Along the route itself, especially once you leave the main settlements, amenities will be very limited, so plan accordingly.

waypoints.name: València d'Àneu Castle ruins and views of the Àneu Valley
